# GitHub Issue Filing Instructions for Dallas

**Generated by:** Keaton (Lead)  
**For:** Dallas (Content Engineer)  
**Date:** 2025-01-28

---

## STEP-BY-STEP: How to Create Issues in GitHub

### Prerequisites
- [ ] Chad has reviewed and approved `concrete-issues-ready-to-file.md`
- [ ] You have GitHub access to TaleLearnCode organization
- [ ] You have write permissions on the chadgreen.com repository

---

## Filing Process

### Step 1: Navigate to GitHub Issues
1. Go to: https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/issues
2. Click **"New issue"** button (green button, top-right)

### Step 2: For Each Issue in `concrete-issues-ready-to-file.md`

**Starting with Issue #1 (Messaging Patterns Cluster):**

1. **Copy the entire issue body** from `concrete-issues-ready-to-file.md`
   - Include everything from `**Title:**` to `**Estimated Effort:**`

2. **In GitHub's "New issue" form:**
   - **Title field:** Copy ONLY the title text (e.g., "[CLUSTER] Extract Messaging Patterns presentation series")
   - **Description field:** Paste the entire **Body** section (everything after `**Body:**`)

3. **Add labels** (right sidebar):
   - Click "Labels" gear icon
   - Add each label listed in the issue (e.g., `squad:dallas`, `content:presentation`, `priority:high`)
   - **Note:** If labels don't exist yet, create them first (see "Label Setup" below)

4. **Assign to yourself:**
   - Click "Assignees" gear icon
   - Select your GitHub username (Dallas)

5. **Set milestone** (optional, if using milestones):
   - Click "Milestone" gear icon
   - Select "Q1 2025 Presentation Backfill" (or create it)

6. **Click "Submit new issue"**

7. **Copy the issue number** that GitHub generates (e.g., #42)
   - Update any dependent issues with this number (see Dependency Map)

8. **Repeat for Issues #2-#10**

---

## Label Setup (Do This FIRST)

Before filing issues, create these labels if they don't exist:

1. Go to: https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/labels
2. Click **"New label"** button
3. Create each label:

| Label Name | Color | Description |
|------------|-------|-------------|
| `squad:dallas` | `#0E8A16` (green) | Assigned to Dallas (Content Engineer) |
| `content:presentation` | `#1D76DB` (blue) | Modifies presentations collection |
| `content:engagementPresentations` | `#5319E7` (purple) | Modifies event mappings |
| `priority:high` | `#D93F0B` (red) | High priority, execute immediately |
| `priority:medium` | `#FBCA04` (yellow) | Medium priority, execute after high |
| `priority:low` | `#C5DEF5` (light blue) | Low priority, backlog |
| `type:workshop` | `#006B75` (teal) | Workshop content requiring special handling |
| `cluster` | `#E99695` (pink) | Multi-repo complexity |
| `batch` | `#BFD4F2` (light blue) | Bulk processing |
| `depends-on-presentations` | `#D4C5F9` (lavender) | Blocked by other presentation issues |
| `private-only` | `#F9D0C4` (peach) | Needs Chad review (private content) |
| `archived` | `#EDEDED` (gray) | Consolidation verification required |

---

## Dependency Handling

### Issue #6 Depends on Issue #7

**After creating Issue #7:**
1. Note the issue number GitHub assigns (e.g., #47)
2. When creating Issue #6, update the dependency section:
   - Change `**Issue #7:**` to `**Issue #47:**` (use actual number)

**How to handle in GitHub:**
- In Issue #6 description, update: "Depends on #47" (use GitHub's auto-linking)
- In Issue #6, add a comment: "⚠️ BLOCKED: Waiting for #47 to complete"
- In Issue #7, add a comment: "🔗 BLOCKS: Issue #6 depends on this"

---

## Execution Order (for Dallas)

After all issues are filed, execute in this order:

### Phase 1: High Priority Standalone (can run in parallel)
1. ✅ **Issue #1:** Messaging Patterns Cluster (5-6 hours)
2. ✅ **Issue #2:** GenAI .NET Developers (35-40 min)
3. ✅ **Issue #3:** Container Apps (35-40 min)
4. ✅ **Issue #4:** Serverless Workshop (70-80 min)
5. ✅ **Issue #5:** EDA Workshop (70-80 min)
6. ✅ **Issue #7:** Serverless Base Presentation (35-40 min) — **MUST complete before #6**

### Phase 2: Event Mapping (dependent)
7. ✅ **Issue #6:** Serverless Event Mapping (25-30 min) — **Requires #7 complete**

### Phase 3: Medium Priority Backfill
8. ✅ **Issue #8:** Event-Driven Microservice Solution (40-45 min)
9. ✅ **Issue #9:** Cloud-Native App (35-40 min)
10. ✅ **Issue #10:** Cosmos DB (35-40 min)

---

## Tracking Progress

### Update issue status as you work:

1. **When starting an issue:**
   - Add label: `status:in-progress`
   - Add comment: "🚀 Started work on {date}"

2. **When blocked:**
   - Add label: `status:blocked`
   - Add comment: "🚧 BLOCKED: {reason}"
   - Tag Keaton or Chad if you need help

3. **When complete:**
   - Add comment: "✅ Complete. Summary: {1-2 sentence summary}"
   - Link to PR or commit (if applicable)
   - Close the issue

4. **If you discover problems:**
   - Add comment describing the issue
   - Tag Keaton (`@keaton`) or Chad (`@chadgreen`) in the comment
   - Add label: `needs-review`

---

## GitHub Markdown Tips

### Checkboxes (Task Lists)
```markdown
- [ ] Task not done
- [x] Task complete
```
You can check/uncheck these directly in GitHub's UI!

### Auto-linking
- `#42` → Automatically links to Issue #42
- `@username` → Tags a user
- Commit SHA → Automatically links to commit

### Code blocks
```markdown
Use backticks for inline code: `src/content/presentations/`
Use triple backticks for code blocks (see issues)
```

---

## Quick Reference Card

**Create new issue:**
```
https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/issues/new
```

**View all labels:**
```
https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/labels
```

**View all issues:**
```
https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/issues
```

**Filter to your issues:**
```
https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+assignee%3A%40me
```

**Filter by label:**
```
https://github.com/TaleLearnCode/chadgreen.com/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3Asquad%3Adallas
```

---

## Common Issues & Solutions

### "I can't find a label"
→ Create it first (see Label Setup section)

### "I accidentally closed an issue"
→ Reopen it: Click "Reopen issue" button at bottom of issue

### "I made a typo in the title"
→ Edit it: Click "Edit" button next to the title

### "I need to update the description"
→ Edit it: Click "..." menu (top-right of description) → "Edit"

### "I'm blocked by another issue"
→ Add comment with `@keaton` or `@chadgreen` tag

### "The issue is more complex than expected"
→ Update the estimated effort in a comment, tag Keaton

---

## Final Checklist Before Filing

- [ ] Chad has approved `concrete-issues-ready-to-file.md`
- [ ] All labels are created in GitHub
- [ ] You have GitHub write access
- [ ] You've read this entire document
- [ ] You understand the dependency between Issue #6 and #7
- [ ] You know how to create, edit, and update issues
- [ ] You know where to ask for help (tag Keaton or Chad)

**Ready to file? Start with Issue #1 (Messaging Patterns Cluster). Good luck! 🚀**
